requirements: Write migration as a single, idempotent forward step plus a usable rollback.
Never DROP or TRUNCATE production tables/columns without a documented deprecation window.
When adding NOT NULL to an existing column, ship the default or backfill first.
Add or update indexes only where the query plan requires them; remove unused indexes.
Wrap multi-step writes in a transaction; pick isolation explicitly when it matters.
Run the migration in a non-production environment with realistic data first.
Update ORM models, fixtures, and seed data to match the new schema.
security: never embed customer data in migration files
ensure new columns holding PII are encrypted/masked per policy
performance: for large tables, prefer concurrent/online migrations or chunked backfills
avoid table rewrites during peak hours
verify: dry-run the migration locally and inspect the resulting schema
run existing tests against the migrated schema
verify rollback path actually restores the previous schema
